old_claim.note: Encryption (Modern Cryptography, Public Key) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: X.509 certificates carry subject public keys and certification paths are validated from trusted CA public keys, making public-key cryptography constitutive of PKI.

source_shape.source_locator: Sections 3.2, 4.1.1.1, 4.1.2.7, and 6.1 define certification paths that bind subjects to public keys and validate from trust-anchor public keys.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: RFC 5280 specifies subject public-key information inside certificates and makes trusted public keys inputs to certification-path validation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the scoped node is X.509 certificate infrastructure, removing public-key cryptography would remove both the certified object and the trust-anchor mechanism rather than merely one implementation option.

ontology_before: Public-key cryptography was treated as one optional enabling route to Public Key Infrastructure despite being the object that X.509 certificates bind and validate.
ontology_after: Public-key cryptography is a required direct prerequisite of the scoped X.509 certificate infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-public-key-infrastructure-x509-boundary: encryption_modern_cryptography_public_key remains required.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped away from public-key certificate infrastructure to a trust system that neither carries nor validates public keys.
short_reason: RFC 5280 makes subject and trust-anchor public keys structural inputs to X.509 certificates and path validation.
